learning_outcomes.description: Argue an independent and defensible design position.
Deploy a reflective and research-driven design process.
Integrate contemporary technology solutions, practice and discourse in the design of a complex design project.
Communicate the experiential qualities and detailed resolution of a design proposal across a range of scales and at exhibition standard.

assessments.assessment_title: Report 1:  Urban Analysis and Community Oriented Development Strategies – Group Assignment
assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Product/ Design, Project
assessments.task_description:
Assessment rationale:

Students will undertake research and analysis activities in teams to identify, select and analyse the chosen precinct through sustainability and community related 'lens'. The report will propose a set of urban development scenarios and design strategies that can be taken forward for the precinct in the second stage of the project.

The output will be an individual report that can incorporate team research and analysis. The report should clearly identify the contributions of team members to the report and presentation.

In-person assessment prohibits use of AI and/or MT: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The School of Architecture, Design and Planning uses Blackboard and Turnitin for assessment submission. Turnitin is accessed through the course Blackboard site. Turnitin also checks for plagiarism or instances where the original work of others is not appropriately acknowledged.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: The late penalty for this assessment item will be calculated as follows:
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Product/ Design, Project
assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale:

Working individually, students are to develop an urban design and development strategy and an implementation plan for their selected suburban area/precinct, which pursues the community-making agendas that have been identified, researched and developed during the semester.

Students are to advance their own ideas on the overall precinct character including relationship existing built form and any new interventions and programmed external space functions to the precinct. The required components of the Strategic Urban Design Framework will be presented and discussed in studio.

In-person assessment prohibits use of AI and/or MT: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The School of Architecture, Design and Planning uses Blackboard and Turnitin for assessment submission. Turnitin is accessed through the course Blackboard site. Turnitin also checks for plagiarism or instances where the original work of others is not appropriately acknowledged.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Studio Sessions

The course will include regular studio sessions to support project-based learning, guide students, and provide feedback on the development of their projects. Studio sessions may involve group discussion, individual consultation, and presentation of work-in-progress. Students are expected to attend studio sessions each week unless advised otherwise. Agendas for each studio session will be advised via Blackboard.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
